Notice Of Motion And Motion To Tax Plaintiffs Cost
Off Calendar Mar-11-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Raymond E. Areshenko, esq. (916-800-6090) on behalf of law offices of Michael Rosenstein for Plaintiff.
Leon Roebinian, esq. (415-717-8657) of Nixon Peabody for Defendants.

RULING - A R G U E D. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTs MICHAEL STEAD'S AUTO DEPOT, INC., and FCA US LLC's Motion To Tax Plaintiff's Cost.

Defendants FCA LLC and Michael Stead's Auto Depot, Inc.'s motion to tax plaintiff's costs is denied. As the prevailing party in this action under the Song-Beverly Act, plaintiff is entitled to recover "a sum equal to the aggregate amount of costs and expenses . . . determined by the court to have been reasonably incurred by the buyer in connection with the commencement and prosecution of such action." (Civ. Code 1794(d).) Under the Song-Beverly Act, the Legislature intended the word "expenses" to cover items, such as expert fees, not included in the statutory definition of "costs" provided by Code of Civil Procedure section 1033.5. (Jensen v. BMW of North America (1995) 35 Cal.App.4th 112, 137-38; see also Warren v. Kia Motors America, Inc. (2018) 30 Cal.App.5th 28, 42-43 [trial court erred in disallowing plaintiff's claim for trial transcripts].) The filing of a verified memorandum of costs is prima facie evidence that listed costs and expenses that appear facially proper were necessarily incurred. Defendants have not met their burden to show that any such costs or expenses were not reasonably or necessarily incurred. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Order is signed in open court.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

MOTION - Notice Of Motion Motion For Attorneys Fees, Costs, And Expenses
Off Calendar Mar-11-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Raymond E. Areshenko, esq. (916-800-6090) on behalf of law offices of Michael Rosenstein for Plaintiff.
Leon Roebinian, esq. (415-717-8657) of Nixon Peabody for Defendants.

RULING - A R G U E D. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF LEANN RICHARDSON's MOTION For Attorneys' Fees, Costs, And Expenses.

Plaintiff Leann M. Richardson's motion for an award of attorneys' fees is granted in the amount of $46,215. The Court finds the amount of time expended on the case and the monetary charge for that time to be excessive and unreasonable, particularly in light of the following factors: (i) the large number of attorneys (eleven, from two different law firms) who billed time on the case, which inevitably involved a great deal of duplication and inefficiency; (ii) the $43,000 in fees incurred by counsel in trial preparation between December 14, 2017, when Defendants made a section 998 offer in the amount of $120,000, and October 9, 2018, when the case settled at a mandatory settlement conference for the modestly higher amount of $127,500; (iii) the large amount of time billed for travel from Southern California to San Francisco, without any clear indication in the billing records as to whether any work on the case was actually performed during those hours; and (iv) the lack of novelty or complexity of the issues, particularly given counsel's subject matter expertise. For these reasons, in order to determine a reasonable award, the Court has reduced the amount of the lodestar by 50%, to $46,215. (See, e.g., Warren v. Kia Motors America, Inc. (2018) 30 Cal.App.5th 24, 37, 40-41 [trial court may apply a negative multiplier to adjust the lodestar figure to arrive at a reasonable fee if it "clearly explain[s] its case-specific reasons for the percentage reduction," which may properly include excessive time spent on case by multiple attorneys from different firms]; Save Our Uniquely Rural Community Environment v. County of San Bernardino (2015) 235 Cal.App.4th 1179, 1188 [trial court did not abuse its discretion in reducing lodestar by 80 percent].) The Court declines to award a positive multiplier. (See Hall v. FCA US LLC (E.D. Cal. May 21, 2018) 2018 WL 2298431 at *8 [declining to award multiplier to plaintiff's counsel in Song-Beverly Act case where, among other things, case did not present novel or difficult questions of law or fact, there was no evidence that the nature of the litigation precluded other employment by the attorneys, and the contingent nature of the fee award was outweighed by the other factors].) (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

Order is signed in open court.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses From Defendant, And Request For Sanctions - First Set Of Request For Production Of Documents
Off Calendar Jun-07-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances in the matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

PLAINTIFF LEANN RICHARDSON Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses From Defendant, And Request For Sanctions - First Set Of Request For Production Of Documents is GRANTED IN PART and DENIED IN PART AS FOLLOWS:
1. DENIED AS TO REQUESTS NOS. 42 - 5L, OBJECTIONS ARE OVERRULED;
2. GRANTED AS TO 61 - 63 TO THE EXTENT THAT DEFENDANT FCA US L.L.C. IS ORDERED TO IDENTIFY THE DOCUMENTS IN THE PETER VELASCO, ET AL. V. CHRYSLER GROUP L.L.C GROUP OF DOCUMENTS THAT REFLECT RESEARCH, TESTING AND ANALYSIS REGARDING THE TIPM-7 WHEITHER PRE-RELEASE OR POST-RELEASE. IT IS NOT A SUFFICENT RESPONSE FOR DEFENDANT TO PRODUCE OVER 100,000 PAGES OF DOCUMENTS AND REQUIRED PLAINTIFF TO DETERMINE WHICH DOCUMENTS MIGHT FALL WITHIN THE SCOPE OF THE REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION. ALL OTHER OBJECTIONS ARE OVERRULED.
3. NO MONETARY SANCTIONS TO BE IMPOSED.
ALL FURTHER RESPONSES SHALL BE SERVED WITHIN THIRTY (30) DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER. COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT WILL PREPARE AN APPROPRIATE ORDER, PRESENT TO COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF FOR APPROVAL AS TO FORM AND SUBMIT TO THE COURT. Judge Pro Tem: Paul A. Renne; Clerk: Felicia Green; Not reported; Dept. 302.
